rationale: The citing paper focuses on Bayesian identification of friend-level peer influence on user log-ins, while the cited paper studies seller-network accessibility effects on social commerce revenues, so their overlap appears broad/topic-level rather than clearly methodological. Because the only linkage evidence is a single DOI-matched reference with no in-text citation context or described use, there is not enough evidence to safely drop as obvious mention-only or to confidently keep as substantive.
